summary: The PoC demonstrates that a custom valueOf method on an object is automatically invoked when the object is used as a Map key, enabling arbitrary code execution.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should create an object with a malicious valueOf method, use it as a Map key, and observe that valueOf() is called during the hashing process, executing attacker-controlled code.
actual_observed_result: The PoC created an object with a custom valueOf method that sets a flag when called. When the object was used as a Map key, valueOf() was invoked automatically, setting the execution flag and demonstrating code execution.
verification_method: The PoC script creates a malicious key object with valueOf that sets a boolean flag. After Map().set(maliciousKey, value), the script checks if the flag was set, confirming that valueOf was called.
evidence_produced_by_run: Console output shows '[PWNED] valueOf() was called!'
The codeExecuted flag was set to true after Map creation
valueOf received the object parameter as expected
Attacker-controlled function executed successfully during hash computation

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
